CHUNK:031/044
Or is God the God of the Jews only? Is he not the God of Gentiles also? Yes, of Gentiles also, since God is one; and he 
will justify the circumcised on the grounds of their faith and the uncircumcised 
through their faith. The fundamental question is that of knowing precisely what it 
means f
or there to be a single God. What does the "mono" in "monothe­
ism" mean? Here Paul confronts-but also renews the terms of
-the f
or­
midable question of the One. His genuinely revolutionary conviction is 
that the sign o
f
 the One is the "for all, " or the "without exce
ption. " That 
there is but a single God must be understood not as a philosophical spec­
ulation concerning substance or the supreme being, but on the basis of a 
structure of address. The One is that which inscribes no difference in the 
subjects to which it addresses itself. The One is only insof
ar as it is for all: 
such is the maxim of universality when it has its root in the event. Monotheism can be understood only by talcing into consideration the 
whole of humanity. Unless addressed to all, the One crumbles and disap­
pears. But, for Paul, the law always designates a particularity, hence a dif­
ference. It is not possible for it to be an operation of the One, because it 
addresses its f
allacious "One" only to those who acknowledge and prac­
tice the injunctions it specifies. The ontological structure underlying this conviction (though Paul 
has no interest whatsoever in ontology) is that no evental One can be the 
One of a particularity. The universal is the only possible correlate f
or the 
One. The general apparatus of a truth contains the One (divine tran­
scendence, monotheism, according to the Pauline f
able), the universal 
(the whole of humanity, both circumcised and uncircumcised), and the 
singular (the Christ-event). The particular, which pertains to opinion, 
custom, law, cannot be inscribed in it. What can measure up to the universality of an address? Not legal­
ity, in any case. The law is always predicative, particular, and partial. Paul is perfectly aware of the law's unfailingly "statist" character. By "sta­
tist" I mean that which enumerates, names, and controls the parts of a 
situation. If a truth is to surge f
orth eventally, it must be nondenumer­
able, impredicable, uncontrollable. This is precisely what Paul calls 
grace: that which occurs without being couched in any predicate, that 


Paul 
Against the Law 
77 
which is translegal, that which happens to everyone without an assigna­
ble reason. Grace is the opposite of law insof
ar as it is what comes with­
out being due. This is a profound insight of Paul's, which, through its universal 
and illegal understanding of the One, undoes every particular or com­
munitarian incorporation of the subject, as well as every juridical or con­
tractual approach to its constitutive division. That which 
founds a sub
ject 
cannot be what is due to it. For this foundation binds itself to that which 
s declared in : radical contingency. If one understands man's humanity 
111
 terms of hIS subjective capacity, there is, strictly speaking, nothing 
whatsoever like a "right" of man. The polemic against the "what is due," against the logic of right 
and duty, lIes at the heart of 
the Pauline refusal of 
works and law: "T
o one 
who works, his wages are not reckoned as a grace but as his due" (Rom. 4·4)· But for Paul, nothing is due. The salvation of the subject cannot 
take. the .form of a reward or wage. The subjectivity of faith is unwaged 
(whIch, 111
 the final analysis, entitles us to call it communist). It pertains 
to the granting of a gift, kharisma. Every subject is initiated on the basis 
?f a charisma;. every subject is charismatic. Since the subjectivating point 
IS the declaratlOn of 
the event, rather than the work that demands a wage 
or r9ward, the declaring subject exists according to the charisma proper 
to hIm. Every subjectivity confronts its division within the element of an 
esse8tial.gratuitousness proper to its purpose. The redemptive operation 
conSIsts 111
 the occurrence of 
a charisma. There is in Paul a fundamental link between universalism and 
charisma, between the One's universal power of 
address, and the absolute 
gratuitousness of militantism. Thus, in Romans 3.22-24, he will state: 
"For there is no distinction [diastole, which means "difference"] , since all 
have sinn7d and f
all short of 
the glory of 
God, by his grace they are justi­
fied as a gIft [dorean], through the redemption which is in Christ Jesus." Dorean is a powerful word; it means "as a pure gift," "without 
cause," and even "in vain." There is for Paul an essential link between the 
"f
or all" of the universal and the "without cause." There is an address for 
all on.ly according to that which is without cause. Only what is absolutely 
gratuItous can be addressed to all. Only charisma and grace measure up 
to a universal problem. Paul Against the Law 
The subject constituted by charisma through the gratuitous prac­
tice of 
the universal address necessarily maintains that there are no diff
er­
ences. Only what is charismatic, thus absolutely without cause, possesses 
this power of being in excess of the law, of collapsing established diff
er­
ences. This is the root of 
the famous Pauline theme concerning the super­
abundance of grace. The law governs a predicative, worldly multiplicity, 
granting to each part of 
the whole its due. Evental grace governs a multi­
plicity in excess of 
itself, one that is indescribable, superabundant relative 
to itself as well as with respect to the fixed distributions of 
the law. The profound ontological thesis here is that universalism supposes 
one be able to think the multiple not as a part, but as in excess of itself
, 
as that which is out of 
place, as a nomadism of 
gratuitousness.

CLAIM: GOD FOR JEWS ONLY? → YES: GENTILES ALSO SINCE GOD=ONE  
STEP: CIRCUMCISED JUSTIFIED BY FAITH ∧ UNCIRCUMCISED JUSTIFIED BY FAITH  
QUESTION: MEANING OF MONO-IN MONOTHEISM  
PAUL: CONFRONTS ∧ RENEWS QUESTION-OF-ONE  
CLAIM: SIGN-OF-ONE=FOR-ALL ∧ WITHOUT-EXCEPTION  
IMPLIES: ONE ≠ PHILOSOPHICAL-SUBSTANCE SPECULATION  
ONE=STRUCTURE-OF-ADDRESS  
CHARACTERISTIC: ONE INSCRIBES NO-DIFFERENCE AMONG ADDRESSEES  
FORMULA: ONE⇔UNIVERSALITY ∧ EVENT-ROOTED  
CONTEXT: MONOTHEISM→WHOLE-HUMANITY  
CONSEQUENCE: ADDRESS≠ALL ⇒ ONE CRUMBLES  
CLAIM: LAW=PARTICULARITY ∧ DIFFERENCE  
REASON: LAW ADDRESSES ONLY OBEYERS-OF-INJUNCTIONS  
THUS: EVENTAL-ONE ≠ PARTICULAR-ONE  
CONCLUSION: UNIVERSAL=ONLY CORRELATE OF ONE  
DEF: TRUTH-APPARATUS={ONE; UNIVERSAL; SINGULAR(CHRIST-EVENT)}  
ASSERT: PARTICULAR(OPINION,CUSTOM,LAW) ¬∈ TRUTH-APPARATUS  
QUESTION: WHAT MEASURES-UP→UNIVERSAL-ADDRESS? NOT LEGALITY  
DEF: LAW=ENUMERATE ∧ NAME ∧ CONTROL PARTS (STATIST)  
REQUIREMENT FOR TRUTH-EVENT: NONDENUMERABLE ∧ IMPREDICABLE ∧ UNCONTROLLABLE  
DEF: GRACE=TRANSLEGAL ∧ NO-PREDICATE ∧ UNIVERSAL-OCCURRENCE ∧ ASSIGNABLE-REASON ¬EXISTS  
PROPERTY: GRACE⇔LAW-OPPOSITE (GRACE COMES-WITHOUT-DUE)  
INSIGHT: UNIVERSAL+ILLEGAL-ONE UNDOES PARTICULAR/COMMUNITARIAN SUBJECT-INCORPORATION  
PREMISE: FOUNDATION-OF-SUBJECT ≠ WHAT-IS-DUE; FOUNDATION=DECLARED RADICAL-CONTINGENCY  
ASSERT: HUMAN-RIGHT ¬EXISTS IF HUMANITY=f(SUBJECTIVE-CAPACITY)  
QUOTE: "TO WORKER, WAGES ¬COUNT AS GRACE BUT AS DUE" [ROM:4.4]  
IMPLIES: NOTHING IS DUE; SALVATION ≠ REWARD/WAGE  
PROPERTY: FAITH-SUBJECTIVITY=UNWAGED (COMMUNIST)  
DEF: KHARISMA=GRATUITOUS-GIFT  
ASSERT: EVERY-SUBJECT INITIATED BY KHARISMA  
ASSERT: SUBJECTIVITY BASED-ON EVENT-DECLARATION NOT WORK-REWARD  
PROPERTY: SUBJECTIVITY FACES DIVISION WITHIN ESSENTIAL-GRATUITOUSNESS  
DEF: REDEMPTIVE-OPERATION=EVENTAL-KHARISMA  
CLAIM: UNIVERSALISM⇔KHARISMA LINK  
CLAIM: ONE-UNIVERSAL-ADDRESS POWER⇔MILITANT-GRATUITOUSNESS  
QUOTE: "NO-DISTINCTION; ALL SINFUL; GLORY-OF-GOD FALLS-SHORT;"  
QUOTE: "JUSTIFIED-BY-GRACE AS DOREAN THROUGH-REDEMPTION-IN-CHRIST-JESUS" [ROM:3.22-24]  
DEF: DOREAN="PURE-GIFT" ∧ "WITHOUT-CAUSE" ∧ "IN-VAIN"  
IMPLIES: FOR-ALL⇔WITHOUT-CAUSE; ONLY ABSOLUTE-GRATUITOUS CAN ADDRESS-ALL  
ASSERT: CHARISMATIC(ABSOLUTE-GRATUITOUS) COLLAPSES LAW-ESTABLISHED DIFFERENCES  
THEME: GRACE-SUPERABUNDANCE  
CONTRAST: LAW=PARTICULATIVE MULTIPLICITY WITH-DUE DISTRIBUTIONS  
GRACE=EVENTAL-MULTIPLICITY IN-EXCESS ∧ SUPERABUNDANT VS LAW  
ONTO-THESIS: UNIVERSALISM⇔THINK MULTIPLE AS {EXCESS-OF-ITSELF; OUT-OF-PLACE}  
ONTO-THESIS: UNIVERSALISM⇔NOMADIC-GRATUITOUSNESS
